title_en: 1. The claim that a number of the Companions abstained from
title_id: 1. Bantahan atas klaim bahwa sejumlah sahabat menahan diri dari membai'at Ali
title_ar: سيرة علي بن أبي طالب — الجزء 48
content_en: swearing allegiance to 'Ali (&) is incorrect because no one refrained from swearing allegiance to him. Some people, including those mentioned above, did not join him; that was an The oath ofallegiance to 'Ali, his characteristics & his way of life issue that is subject to individual opinion, so each individual worked it out for himself and acted accordingly.58 With regard to Ibn Khaldoon's comment that the people were scattered throughout the regions at the time of 'UthmSn's murder and were not present when the oath of allegiance was sworn to 'Ali (&), and that of those who were present, some swore allegiance and some (Sa'd, Sa'eed, Ibn 'Umar and others)59 refrained until the people gathered and agreed on a leader, what is mentioned is an exaggeration on the part of Ibn Khaldoon (may AUah have mercy on him). With regard to Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas, his swearing allegiance to 'Ali (&) was narrated by Ibn Sa'd, Ibn Wbbih, adh-Dhahabi and others.60 The same is tme for the rest; they swore allegiance, as we have mentioned, and there was consensus on that among the Companions who were present in Madinah. Ibn Khaldoon himself has narrated that there was consensus among the second generation after the Companions in Madinah that the caliphate of 'Ali (&) became valid and was binding on all Muslims. I have quoted what Ibn Khaldoon said because many writers and researchers relied on it later on.
content_id:
Klaim bahwa sejumlah sahabat menahan diri dari membai'at Ali radhiyallahu 'anhu adalah tidak benar, karena tidak ada seorang pun yang menahan diri dari membai'atnya. Sebagian orang, termasuk mereka yang telah disebutkan di atas, tidak bergabung bersamanya dalam beberapa pertempuran; itu adalah masalah yang tunduk pada pendapat ijtihad individu, sehingga masing-masing individu menentukan pilihan sendiri berdasarkan pertimbangannya dan bertindak sesuai dengan itu.

Adapun komentar Ibn Khaldun bahwa orang-orang tersebar di berbagai daerah pada saat terbunuhnya 'Utsman radhiyallahu 'anhu dan tidak hadir ketika baiat diberikan kepada Ali radhiyallahu 'anhu, dan bahwa di antara yang hadir, sebagian membai'at dan sebagian (Sa'd, Sa'id, Ibn Umar, dan lainnya) menahan diri hingga orang-orang berkumpul dan sepakat atas seorang pemimpin, apa yang disebutkan adalah sebuah berlebih-lebihan dari pihak Ibn Khaldun rahimahullah. Adapun Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqash, pembai'atannya kepada Ali radhiyallahu 'anhu telah diriwayatkan oleh Ibn Sa'd, Ibn Wabih, adz-Dzahabi, dan lainnya. Hal yang sama berlaku untuk yang lainnya; mereka membai'at, sebagaimana telah kami sebutkan, dan terdapat ijma' atas hal itu di antara para sahabat yang hadir di Madinah. Ibn Khaldun sendiri telah meriwayatkan bahwa terdapat ijma' di antara generasi tabi'in di Madinah bahwa kekhalifahan Ali radhiyallahu 'anhu menjadi sah dan mengikat bagi seluruh kaum muslimin. Aku mengutip apa yang Ibn Khaldun katakan karena banyak penulis dan peneliti kemudian bergantung padanya, padahal pandangan tersebut tidak sepenuhnya akurat sebagaimana telah dijelaskan oleh para ulama hadis dan sejarah.
content_ar: swearing allegiance to 'علي (&) is incorrect لأن no one refrained from swearing allegiance to him. Some people, بما في ذلك those mentioned above, did not join him; that was an The oath ofallegiance to 'علي, his characteristics & his الطريق of الحياة issue that is subject to individual opinion, لذا each individual worked it out for himself and acted وبناءً على ذلك.58 فيما يتعلق بـ Ibn Khaldoon's comment that the people were scattered throughout the regions at the time of 'UthmSn's murder and were not present عندما the oath of allegiance was sworn to 'علي (&), and that of those who were present, some swore allegiance and some (Sa'd, Sa'eed, ابن عمر and others)59 refrained حتى the people gathered and agreed on a leader, what is mentioned is an exaggeration on the part of Ibn Khaldoon (may AUah have الرحمة on him). فيما يتعلق بـ Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas, his swearing allegiance to 'علي (&) was narrated by Ibn Sa'd, Ibn Wbbih, adh-Dhahabi and others.60 The same is tme for the rest; they swore allegiance, as we have mentioned, and there was consensus on that among the الصحابة who were present in المدينة المنورة. Ibn Khaldoon himself has narrated that there was consensus among the ثانياً generation بعد the الصحابة in المدينة المنورة that the الخلافة of 'علي (&) became valid and was binding on all المسلمون. I have quoted what Ibn Khaldoon said لأن many writers and researchers relied on it later on.
